p3.title: requested_pack_id (CP00-093) differs from planned/actual pack_id (CP00-094)
p3.detail: Header lists requested_pack_id CP00-093 while planned_pack_id and pack_id are CP00-094. CP00-093 is already closed (recent commit 'Close CP00-093 review closeout finding routing map pack') and is bound here strictly as the consumed upstream. The planner correctly advanced to the terminal pack CP00-094; the assembler fail-closes on CP00-093 pack drift and missing CP00-093 finding-routing completion, so the upstream is consumed, not re-closed.
p3.resolution: Reconciled, informational only. No action required; recommend recording the request->plan advance in docs/closeout-packs/cp00-094/manifest.json for traceability.

risk_sized_pack_scope.evidence: Single C-class closeout pack binds all 24 planned units across RP00.P09.M06-M10 (range RP00.P09.M06.S01-RP00.P09.M10.S01) via terminal_unit_matrix + micro_phase_closeout_map; override_reason 'RP00 terminal closeout C pack' present; no 1-unit fragmentation. Contract marks the same 24 CP00-094 units production_ready and validator adds those 24 to requiredClosedSubphases.

metadata_only_no_write_boundary.evidence: Policy forbids runtime Claude review, review-queue/assignment/notification writes, human-approval synth/write/bypass, runtime permission/AuthZ/security-trimming, audit/event writes, Hermes/command-evidence writes, DB/storage/product-state/idempotency/lock writes, LDIP impl, HRX split, and premature product completion. Assembler fail-closes on unsafe-forbidden and unknown-truthy claims; validators assert no-write/no-runtime flags; module-no-write test plus 6/6 'Review Closeout Terminal Handoff' tests pass; node --check exit 0 on all sources.

upstream_cp00_093_enforced.evidence: Policy binds CP00-093 required input fields and CP00-093 evidence refs; assembler fail-closes on CP00-093 pack drift and on missing CP00-093 finding-routing completion before emitting the handoff; policy-binding and fail-closed-upstream tests cover this path.

rp00_to_rp01_handoff_not_product_complet.evidence: rp00_closeout_contract sets next RP01.P00.M00.S01; policy forbids premature product-candidate completion; assembler fail-closes on next_subphase drift; contract current_subphase and validator cursor both set to terminal RP00.P09.M10.S01 (RP00 boundary), not a final-product/goal-complete state.

hrx_ldip_boundaries.evidence: rp00_closeout_contract preserves 54,355 Law Firm OS + 901 embedded HRX = 55,256 expanded total and LDIP as internal planning-gated capability; policy forbids HRX product split and LDIP implementation; contract-mirror test validates these invariants. Arithmetic checks out (54,355 + 901 = 55,256).

fixture_contract_validator_consistency.evidence: Fixture control_plane_review_closeout_terminal_handoff is generated from the assembler; contract adds CP00-094 definition/result and 24 production_ready units; validator adds the 24 units to requiredClosedSubphases, moves cursor to RP00.P09.M10.S01, maps CP00-094 construction inspection to terminal S01, and requires control_plane_review_closeout_terminal_handoff_verified; fixture-mirror and contract-mirror tests pass; all node --check exit 0. The npm run rp00:control-plane:validate exit 1 is the expected pre-evidence state (only missing docs/closeout-packs/cp00-094/{manifest,command-evidence,claude-review-result,adjudication,construction-inspection} artifacts that this review and its siblings produce), not a code/consistency defect.

parsed_review.summary: CP00-094 is a correctly risk-sized terminal RP00 closeout pack binding all 24 planned units (RP00.P09.M06-M10) as one C-class pack rather than arbitrary 1-unit closeouts. It preserves the metadata-only/no-write/no-runtime boundary via an explicit forbid list, fail-closed assembler, and no-write/no-runtime validators (6/6 tests pass, all node --check exit 0). Upstream CP00-093 finding-routing completion is enforced (fail-close on pack drift and missing completion) before the handoff. RP00 completion is scoped to the RP00->RP01 terminal handoff with next_subphase RP01.P00.M00.S01 and explicit forbidding of premature product completion, not final product/goal closeout. HRX-embedded (901, no split) and LDIP planning-gated (no implementation) boundaries are preserved, and fixture/contract/validator are mutually consistent. No P0/P1/P2 findings; one P3 informational note that requested_pack_id CP00-093 differs from planned/actual CP00-094 (reconciled: CP00-093 is the already-closed upstream consumed here). The sole validate failure is the expected missing docs/closeout-packs/cp00-094/* evidence artifacts this review produces; it does not block closeout once written. Verdict PASS_WITH_FINDINGS; does not block pack or goal closeout.
